title: show uid_map in podman info
body:
show uid_map in podman info
close #4067 
Signed-off-by: Qi Wang <qiwan@redhat.com>

comments.body: How does the output look when you run podman info?  Are the line separations ok?

> How does the output look when you run podman info? Are the line separations ok?

It directly prints out the IDMap struct
```
$ podman info
host:
  ... 
  IDMappings:
    gidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1000
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
    uidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1000
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
  MemFree: 1369817088
  ...
```

The output looks good, but I'm concerned that if there are several hundred or more mappings on a system, then `podman info` will have an enormous amount of output.  Could we create a '--quiet'  option for `podman info` that would not show all of this, or at that time perhaps just show a count for the number of uid-map entries found?  Maybe instead have a `--verbose` option that would only show this info at that time?  @mheon WDYT?
Code LGTM other than my voluminous output concern.
